methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
C12 Quantum Electronics is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Matthieu and Pierre Desjardins that has raised $19.4M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 known comparables: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti, D-Wave, and Infleqtion.

Due to limited same-stage and adjacent-stage peers with comparable financials, the peer-median method was not applicable. Instead, the valuation relies on stage-progression analysis, applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to seed-stage transitions. Using the $19.4M total raised as a valuation proxy yields an implied value of $39M at the next stage. This method reflects the risk-adjusted capital deployment pattern observed across frontier technology rounds.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $33M to $45M, with a median of $39M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological approach rather than robust triangulation. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable Series A quantum computing transactions with disclosed valuations, making stage-progression assumptions material to the conclusion. Investors should note that additional market data on peer financials and recent quantum computing equity raises would substantially improve estimate confidence.
